Treats.label: Treats
Treats.description: Therapeutic Application: A drug, medical procedure, or therapeutic intervention (Subject) is clinically administered to cure, manage, or alleviate a disease or phenotype (Object). [Direction: Intervention -> Disease]

Causes.label: Causes
Causes.description: Etiological Causation: An external agent, pathogen, chemical, or specific behavior (Subject) is the direct initiating cause of a disease or physiological condition (Object). [Direction: Cause -> Effect]

Associated-With.label: Associated-With
Associated-With.description: Statistical/Clinical Correlation: A general, non-causal, or bidirectional correlation exists between two entities where explicit directionality or mechanism is undefined or ambiguous in the context. [Direction: Symmetric/Undirected]
Associated-With.example: Hypertension is associated with Obstructive Sleep Apnea.

Expressed-In.label: Expressed-In
Expressed-In.description: Biological Localization: A gene, protein, or molecular entity (Subject) is synthesized or functionally present within a specific tissue, cell type, or anatomical structure (Object). [Direction: Molecule -> Location]
Expressed-In.example: Insulin is expressed in Pancreatic Beta Cells.

Located-In.label: Located-In
Located-In.description: Anatomical Containment: A biological structure, lesion, or pathological finding (Subject) is spatially situated within a larger anatomical body part or region (Object). [Direction: Sub-structure -> Super-structure]
Located-In.example: Deep Vein Thrombosis is located in the Lower Extremity.

Part-Of.label: Part-Of
Part-Of.description: Meronymy: An entity (Subject) is a physical component or constituent element of a larger biological system or structure (Object). [Direction: Component -> Whole]
Part-Of.example: The Alveolus is part of the Lung.

Subtype-Of.label: Subtype-Of
Subtype-Of.description: Taxonomic Hierarchy: An entity (Subject) is a specific category or child concept of a broader biological or medical class (Object). [Direction: Child -> Parent]
Subtype-Of.example: Glioblastoma Multiforme is a subtype of Astrocytoma.

Interacts-With.label: Interacts-With
Interacts-With.description: Molecular Interaction: A chemical or biological molecule (Subject) physically or functionally engages with another molecule (Object) without a clearly defined directional dominance. [Direction: Symmetric/Undirected]

Inhibits.label: Inhibits
Inhibits.description: Negative Regulation: A chemical substance or biological molecule (Subject) decreases, suppresses, or blocks the biological activity, expression, or function of a target molecule or pathway (Object). [Direction: Regulator -> Target]

Activates.label: Activates
Activates.description: Positive Regulation: A chemical substance or biological molecule (Subject) increases, stimulates, or upregulates the biological activity, expression, or function of a target molecule or pathway (Object). [Direction: Regulator -> Target]

Diagnoses.label: Diagnoses
Diagnoses.description: Diagnostic Utility: A specific test, imaging modality, or assay (Subject) is utilized to confirm or detect the presence of a disease or condition (Object). [Direction: Tool -> Disease]

Has-Symptom.label: Has-Symptom
Has-Symptom.description: Phenotypic Manifestation: A disease or pathological condition (Subject) manifests clinically through a specific observable symptom or sign (Object). [Direction: Disease -> Phenotype]

Used-For.label: Used-For
Used-For.description: General Indication: A medical device, substance, or object (Subject) is utilized for a broad medical purpose or procedure (Object), distinguishable from treating a specific disease. [Direction: Item -> Purpose]
Used-For.example: Catheters are used for Urinary Drainage.

Contraindicated-For.label: Contraindicated-For
Contraindicated-For.description: Medical Restriction: A drug or procedure (Subject) must not be administered due to the presence of a specific disease, condition, or patient state (Object). [Direction: Drug -> Condition]

Biomarker-Of.label: Biomarker-Of
Biomarker-Of.description: Diagnostic Indicator: A measurable molecule or genetic characteristic (Subject) serves as a quantifiable indicator for the presence or severity of a disease state (Object). [Direction: Molecule -> Disease]
Biomarker-Of.example: PSA (Prostate-Specific Antigen) is a biomarker of Prostate Cancer.

Agonism-or-Antagonism.label: Agonism-or-Antagonism
Agonism-or-Antagonism.description: Receptor Modulation: A drug or ligand (Subject) binds to a receptor (Object) to trigger (agonist) or block (antagonist) a biological response. Used when specific direction is abstract. [Direction: Ligand -> Receptor]

Binding.label: Binding
Binding.description: Physical Attachment: A molecule (Subject) physically attaches to a specific site on another molecule (Object) with high affinity. [Direction: Ligand -> Target]

Affects-Expression.label: Affects-Expression
Affects-Expression.description: Gene Regulation: A drug, chemical, or upstream gene (Subject) alters the transcription or translation levels of a downstream gene or protein (Object). [Direction: Regulator -> Gene/Protein]
Affects-Expression.example: Dexamethasone affects expression of Anti-inflammatory genes.

Metabolizes.label: Metabolizes
Metabolizes.description: Metabolic Conversion: An enzyme or biological system (Subject) chemically transforms a drug or substrate (Object). [Direction: Enzyme -> Substrate]

Transports.label: Transports
Transports.description: Molecular Transport: A protein or transporter channel (Subject) facilitates the movement of a solute or ion (Object) across a biological membrane. [Direction: Transporter -> Solute]

Enzyme-Activity.label: Enzyme-Activity
Enzyme-Activity.description: Activity Modulation: A substance (Subject) specifically impacts the catalytic rate or functional efficiency of an enzyme (Object). [Direction: Modulator -> Enzyme]
Enzyme-Activity.example: Allopurinol reduces Xanthine Oxidase enzyme activity.

Side-Effect.label: Side-Effect
Side-Effect.description: Adverse Reaction: A pharmaceutical agent or treatment (Subject) unintentionally causes a harmful or unwanted phenotype or condition (Object). [Direction: Drug -> Phenotype]

Contributes-To-Pathogenesis.label: Contributes-To-Pathogenesis
Contributes-To-Pathogenesis.description: Pathogenic Mechanism: A biological process, pathway, or molecular event (Subject) is a component of the mechanism driving the development or progression of a disease (Object). [Direction: Mechanism -> Disease]
Contributes-To-Pathogenesis.example: Amyloid Beta accumulation contributes to pathogenesis of Alzheimer's Disease.

Mutation-Affects-Disease.label: Mutation-Affects-Disease
Mutation-Affects-Disease.description: Genetic Risk: A specific genetic variant or mutation (Subject) is causally linked to an increased risk or modulation of a disease phenotype (Object). [Direction: Variant -> Disease]
Mutation-Affects-Disease.example: BRCA1 Mutation affects Breast Cancer risk.

Gene-Regulates-Gene.label: Gene-Regulates-Gene
Gene-Regulates-Gene.description: Gene Regulatory Network: An upstream gene or transcription factor (Subject) controls the expression or function of a downstream gene (Object). [Direction: Regulator -> Target Gene]
